message:
feat(doctor,security): tmux session name injection prevention, artifact path traversal hardening, flexible capture driver dependencies

Tmux session name injection prevention (security):
- New tmux_attach_command_literal() in util.rs properly shell-quotes
  session names via shell_single_quote()
- All prior format!("tmux attach-session -t {session_name}") calls in
  capture.rs, doctor.rs, report.rs, and runmeta.rs replaced with the
  safe helper
- tmux_attach_command field removed from AppSmokeResult and
  DoctorSummaryInputs structs -- now derived on the fly from
  tmux_session so a malicious tmux_attach_command value in a run_meta
  JSON cannot inject arbitrary shell commands
- Tests verify that a stored "echo pwned" attack payload in
  tmux_attach_command is ignored in favor of the properly quoted
  regenerated value

Artifact path traversal hardening (security):
- New resolve_run_artifact_path() in capture.rs ensures output/snapshot
  paths stay within the run directory
- retain_run_scoped_artifact_path() in runmeta.rs now rejects relative
  paths containing . or .. components and adds
  canonicalize_run_scoped_candidate() to safely handle not-yet-existing
  paths by walking up to an existing ancestor
- Artifact entries and replay commands resolve relative paths against
  run_dir instead of trusting raw strings from metadata
- write_artifact_manifest() falls back to inside the run directory when
  configured paths escape it

Capture driver dependency flexibility (usability):
- doctor.rs replaces hard check_command("vhs") + check_command("ttyd")
  with check_capture_driver_dependencies() that accepts Docker as an
  alternative to host vhs+ttyd
- capture.rs skips vhs/docker requirement checks during --dry-run
- Integration tests updated: renamed missing_vhs to
  missing_capture_driver, added skip_if_missing_doctor_capture_stack()
  helper, added dry_run and docker acceptance tests, removed
  unnecessary vhs skip guards from dry-run tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
